name: config-hmcl-ae
description: How to configure HMCL-AE itself — add model providers/models, MCP servers, skills, and web search by editing the AE JSON config files. Use whenever the user asks to configure the AI assistant, add a model/provider, set up an MCP server (e.g. "configure tavily-mcp"), install a skill/skill-store (e.g. "install SkillHub, CLI only"), or enable web search.
body:
# Configuring HMCL-AE (the AI assistant configures itself)

HMCL-AE stores its own configuration as JSON files/folders under the launcher home directory
**`.hmcl/`**. To change it: `read` the relevant file first (so you preserve existing entries),
then `edit` or `write` it. Most changes take effect after the user reopens AI Settings or
restarts HMCL; skills need a rescan ("重新扫描技能目录" in AI Settings → 技能).

## File map (all under `.hmcl/`)

| Concern | File | Shape |
|---|---|---|
| Providers + global LLM params | `ai-settings.json` | object |
| MCP servers | `ai-mcp-settings.json` | **array** |
| Web search | `ai-search-settings.json` | object |
| Tool permissions | `ai-tool-permissions.json` | object |
| Skills | `ai-skills/<name>/SKILL.md` | folder + markdown |

Gotchas:
1. `ai-settings.json` provider `apiKey` is Base64-encoded on disk; `ai-search-settings.json`
   `apiKey` is plain text. Encode with `shell`: PowerShell
   `[Convert]::ToBase64String([Text.Encoding]::UTF8.GetBytes('sk-xxx'))`; on macOS/Linux (bash/zsh)
   `printf '%s' 'sk-xxx' | base64` (use `base64 -w0` on Linux if the output must stay on one line).
2. `ai-mcp-settings.json` is an array; the rest are objects.

For exact field shapes and examples, read the target file itself — existing entries are the
best template. For a new MCP server, `web_fetch` its README/manifest first to get the right
command/url/env. For a skill install from a URL, `web_fetch` the manifest, follow it, and
`write` `SKILL.md` (+ companions) under `.hmcl/ai-skills/<name>/`; install only the subset the
user asked for (e.g. "CLI only").

## After any change

State which file(s) changed and what was added; remind the user to reopen AI Settings (or
restart HMCL; rescan for skills). Never invent API keys — ask if one is required and missing.
